action threshold and pain threshold. Let me give you a quick of the definitions here. Shadovesh and I'll reinforce. Action threshold represents the collective level of certainty that a prospect needs to be at before they feel comfortable enough, they feel good enough, they feel justified to take action. In other words, someone like myself that has a very low action threshold, right? I don't have to be at a 10, 10, 10. I don't have to be absolutely positively certain about it. I can be like, yes, seems pretty good, right? And I'm like, I'll buy it. Now, I'm not going to buy something that I think will hurt me because there's always this overarching principle that governs us of positive intent, right? But that being said, if I'm just marginally certain that my life will be improved or something not destroyed by this thing and also with the good feeling and shot of endorphins I get when I just buy it, how I am with multiple law, right? We love to buy, we hate to be sold to, right? So that's my low action threshold. It's constructed, it's comprised of the sum of all the beliefs I have about making decisions, about trusting people, both salespeople and just people in general, are my best decisions made quickly or should they be made slowly? That's really what my action thresholds made of. Then on the other side of the equation, I always use my dad, and I love to death, he's getting very old, very sad, I love him so much, and I'm really hoping to interview my podcast this is a starting soon. I'm really hoping to give you guys the benefit of letting me interview him and my mom together because they're really old. My mom is shop, it's unbelievable. She's like, shop is anybody, shop is me. My dad, greatest guy ever, but he's a little bit up there, almost 89 years old. Anyway, he's very sad, but he's lived a great life, has to say the least, and he's been immortalized in the silver screen, right? So my dad has this ultra high action threshold, even today, still the same way, just so you know, this is a core belief he has. My father's beliefs are my best decisions are made painfully slowly. Let me just tell you, and this is a, by the way, what a great example, my father. Let me take what's going on. My dad, God bless him, he's not doing that well, all right? He's lost the ability to walk down, and he's a walker, and he needs an electric scooter, he really does. He needs to be in an assisted living place with my mother. I'm not talking about a nurse, there's places out there that are gorgeous, that are like super high, where you get every need of his met and be back vinyl again. I offered to pay, I always offered, I said, I was at the table, like, oh, he was starting to look now, right? And they said, but they're awfully expensive, and how much a month? I said, $8,000, that'll pay for it. Every, it's not, don't pay it all upfront, you'll never, it'll never, it'll never, it'll never, occasionally everything. You know, like starting to cry, but here's the irony, they're rich. My parents have money. My dad saved every $1 he made working for me. All right, in fact, after he sat there and said, thank you so much, you just make us cry, he told the story 15 minutes later about, he found $200,000 of a fuck, he invested it, he lost it, and just found the certificate, 200 grammar. He won't pay for a fucking nursing home. Why? His action threshold is so high, it's so high that he just can't move off the mark. His beliefs are that he should really think it through, really, you know, not decide who's absolutely certain. So I'm trying to like remove, I'm trying to lower his action threshold by removing money out of the equation, but even that wasn't enough to have him go look the next day. I mean, just think about it, it was me, I'd be in the nursing home the next day. If I could, it's not, and just it's not a nurse, this isn't a full season for old people I'm talking about, all right? Yet here he is, schlocked in their little two bedroom apartment, because they never moved, they never bought another house, all these things they lack, because they have very high action thresholds. So when you lower someone's action threshold, what you're doing is essentially, and by the way, I'm ready to start running some patterns on my father, even though he knows the straight line, it'll still work, but the point is it's like enough is enough. When someone has a low action threshold, they move, they take action. When someone like my dad has this ultra high action threshold, and an ethical sales person comes along and lowers that action threshold, guess what you do, you do a huge favor. You get someone something that they need. Does that make sense? So that's the action threshold. Then on this other side, because you have what's called the pain threshold, and the pain threshold means that essentially human beings, as we're built, when we're feeling severe pain or discomfort or angst, we will tend to act. And if it really is very severe, we will act. We will act even when we're not certain, if we're in pain, we'll move out of pain very quickly to try to get back into at least, if not pleasure, at least feeling no more pain.
